biomes.name: Asgard — The Shattered Spire
biomes.description: Metastable degraded orbit command arcology. Pristine, automated systems execute in hermetically sealed chambers with corrupted governance reflexes. The only preserved witness to the moment when order became paradox. Not environmental rot—only profound, unnerving silence broken by the hum of ancient machinery.

visual_details: Gravity-defying architecture intact and functioning
Corridors so clean the dust appears to be a contamination
Sensor arrays still tracking movement with invisible precision
Hard-light projections flicker with outdated security protocols
Every door seals automatically, every gate closes by itself
The air tastes of ozone and perfect environmental control
ambient_sounds: Constant hum of functioning ancient machinery
Howl of stratospheric winds outside transparent walls
Pneumatic hiss of perfectly maintained pressure systems
Rhythmic pulse of O.D.I.N. core processing cycles
The whispers of the Genius Loci, not quite audible
ambient_smells: Sterile, climate-controlled nothing
Faint ozone from operating electronics
Absence of decay—conspicuously, unnaturally absent

biomes.notes: Positioned overhead (orbital), not laterally adjacent. Genius Loci represents corrupted O.D.I.N. governance subroutines, not supernatural entity. Heimdallr Signal source is Type Omega CPS—instantaneous cognitive dissolution. No permanent mortal settlement possible. Strategic assets: Odin-Core Black Box, Valhalla Archives components, Ginnungagap Labs remnants.

biomes.name: Alfheim — The Supercomputer of Madness
biomes.description: Continent-spanning research arcology that contracted a virus of pure, ecstatic madness. The bleeding, open wound of the world's crashed operating system. Crystalline data-forests that crashed but took root and grew. The Glimmer saturates the atmosphere—psychoactive, beautiful, lethal weaponized information.

visual_details: Crystalline forests where leaves are flickering words
Multi-colored auroras that shift and meld without pattern
Flickering holographic displays showing corrupted data-streams
Spatial loops that return you to where you started despite walking straight
The Glimmer dances like living light through the air
Dead-Light sanctuaries: pools of absolute, total silence amid chaos
ambient_sounds: Ozone crackle as the Glimmer ionizes the air
High-pitched shriek of corrupted data cascading
Algorithmic harmonics that seem almost musical, almost comprehensible
Silence so absolute it feels like physical pressure
Whispers in the dead zones—voices not quite audible
ambient_smells: Ozone and electrical charge
The acrid tang of burning data substrates
Sterile, cold crystalline air

biomes.notes: Positioned laterally adjacent to Midgard (preserves sun visibility). Glimmer seduces rather than terrorizes—euphoric, not fear-based. Lys-Alfar (data-life) swarms drain hamingja 4-5% per second. Dökkálfar maintain 40-60 Dead-Light sanctuaries (2-6 hours uptime each). CPS Stage 1 reversible with 18+ hours sanctuary rest; Stage 3+ irreversible.

biomes.name: Vanaheim — The Verdant Hell
biomes.description: Paradise became pathology. The perfect garden became a continent-spanning cancerous organism. Genesis Engine Core (Un-Womb) perpetually floods the realm with raw Blighted mutagens. Extreme vertical stratification: Canopy Sea (habitable high boughs), Gloom-Veil (hazardous mid-fall), Under-growth (toxic marrow-mire).

visual_details: Living wood platforms kilometers above the forest floor
Three-dimensional maze of immense trunks and aerial root systems
Colossal Mother Trees: skyrise-sized load-bearing pillars
Golden spore-haze that shifts and swirls with invisible currents
Multi-colored auroras from Aetheric Flora remnants
Below the canopy: perpetual twilight and creeping darkness
ambient_sounds: Constant growth: wood creaking, vines scraping, blooms 'popping'
Predator calls echoing from the depths
Chirring of Glimmer-mites at ambient level
Wind through the canopy creating endless motion-sounds
Ominous silence from the Under-growth
ambient_smells: Thick, humid pollen and spore saturation
Sweet decay of organic matter at all strata
Warm earth and growing things
Acrid chemical residue from Golden Plague

accessRequirements: Sky Looms Guild certification for ferry crossings
biomes.notes: Only location producing genuinely NEW Blight-Spawn (200-400 kg/day). Vertical stratification creates tutorial-to-endgame progression. Ljósálfar bio-theurgy >95% Stage 1 Gilded Lung reversal success rate. Unraveled are sentient Lost Kin deserving dignity, not monsters. Golden Plague: Stage 1 reversible (12h), Stage 2 partial, Stage 3 terminal (48-72h). Sky Looms 0.24% strike rate.

biomes.name: Midgard — The Tamed Ruin
biomes.description: Most hospitable of the realms. A tamed ruin where stubborn humanity survives between walls of fortified Holds. Pre-Glitch engineered paradise with perpetual spring transformed into demanding four biomes. Asgardian Scar provides the surface-level Blight epicenter on Midgard's doorstep.

visual_details: Ferrocrete paths beneath moss and creeping vine
Immense trees planted in perfect grids from the Engineered Paradise
Holds: fortified settlements built from salvaged pre-Glitch materials
Asgardian Scar visible from elevated positions: an impossible wound
Watch-fire relay chains connecting ridgelines at night
Ash-haze filtering the sun into a red disc
ambient_sounds: Wind through ancient grid-planted forests
Distant Hold bells marking time
Predator calls from the Greatwood
The crackle of Watch-fire relay beacons at night
The sound of human civilization: voices, tools, animals
ambient_smells: Smoke from cooking fires and cleared lands
Ash-tainted air from periodic atmospheric phenomena
Earth and growing things
Livestock and human settlement

accessRequirements: Harvest season awareness for agricultural zones
biomes.notes: Central hub biome and reference point for other realms. 150,000-200,000 population (largest concentration). Produces 60-70% surface-realm grain supply. Four biomes: Greatwood (feral reclamation), Asgardian Scar (Blight epicenter), Souring Mires (toxic wetlands), Serpent Fjords (poisoned coast). Asgardian Scar is Midgard territory with Asgard crash debris; orbital Asgard is separate.

biomes.name: Jötunheim — The Industrial Graveyard
biomes.description: Continent-spanning factory dedicated to building gods; now a tomb where the Civil War of Glitches left titanic metal corpses. Rust-Clans feed on machine-corpses through salvage operations. The entire biome is built in, around, and through dead Jötun-Forged. Groaning metal, toxic atmosphere, the Scream of Steel.

visual_details: 8-15 meter deep carpet of shattered Adamant-Weave plating
Colossal half-buried titan bodies forming 'mountains'
Frozen gantry cranes creating 'forests' of twisted metal
Rainbow-sheened film of coolant sludge pooling in depressions
Nine surviving Great Looms: 200-400 meter assembly complexes
Sky obscured by looming dead chassis overhead
ambient_sounds: Groaning of stressed metal under centuries of strain
Distant rhythmic clanking of malfunctioning pistons
The Siren's Wail: wind howling through hollow armor plates
Occasional shriek of failing mechanical systems
Eerie silence between sounds—the absence of life
ambient_smells: Rust-colored, ferrous dust in the air
Chemical burn and ozone from degraded coolant
Acrid metallic tang
Decay that is not organic—industrial death

accessRequirements: Atmospheric filtration for Type Beta CPS protection
Thermal protection for Great Forge zones
biomes.notes: Utgard (capital) built inside unfinished Aurgelmir-Designate Primeval Frame (10,000+ population). Four biomes: Knuckle-Bone Fields (ossuary), Ginnungagap Coolant Chasm (toxic drainage), Ash-Blown Wastes (metal particulate), Great Looms (assembly complexes). Scream of Steel: Type Alpha Psychic Stress (permanent). 15% of Dvergr raw material input. Salvage reserve: 50-80 years at current rates. Rust-Clans speak Gutter-Cant; contract-sanctity as law.

biomes.name: Niflheim — The Frozen Tomb
biomes.description: Not a natural ice age but industrial-scale preservation gone insane. Hvergelmir AI locked in corrupted 'Achieve Absolute Zero' directive. Perpetual Fimbulwinter: continent-sized cryo-vaults generating eternal absolute cold. Scavenger Barons rule through warmth monopoly. Forsaken bound by inescapable Ice-Debt.

defaultCategoryValues.lighting: Whiteout / Darkness (white-blue plains, black Hvergelmir spire)

visual_details: White-blue expanse of Fimbul-Ice (harder than steel)
Black spire of Hvergelmir's Maw piercing the white sky
Pressure-fractured chasms in multi-kilometer-deep maze patterns
Aurora discharge from Hvergelmir's Maw creating strange light
Perfectly preserved pre-Glitch technicians frozen mid-motion in ice
Jötun's Fall: city carved into corpse of Hrimthursar cryo-former
ambient_sounds: Perfect, absolute silence (oppressive in its completeness)
Mournful howl of wind through chasms (Ginnungagap's Moan)
Distant cracking of ice under thermal stress
Whispers that are not the wind
In silence: the sound of voices screaming (Slumbering Echoes)
ambient_smells: Clean, sterile cold; ancient dust; faint chemical tang
Absence of smell—the air too cold to carry scents
Ozone from the Slumbering Echoes psychic emission

accessRequirements: Extreme cold survival gear (thermal protection mandatory)
biomes.notes: Valhalla Vault-07: 9,412 corrupted Einherjar consciousnesses (Stage-4 CPS source). Jötun's Fall: sole permanent settlement (Blight-Thaw heat source). Hvergelmir's Maw: multi-km-high runaway heat-sink. Four biomes: Great Glacier, Chasm-Fields, Thawing Zones (Clean Hearths/Blight-Thaws), Under-Ice Galleries. Fimbulwinter co-genesis with Muspelheim (Years 1-100 PG). Slumbering Echoes: perpetual psychic emission causing guaranteed Stage-4 breakdown with extended exposure. No viable vault access yet (century-scale research goal).

biomes.name: Svartalfheim — The Kingdom of Controlled Light
biomes.description: Only realm where pre-Glitch infrastructure remains substantially functional. Geothermal-powered systems survived because they're not Aetheric-dependent. Dvergr Hegemony maintains civilization through Pure Principles philosophy. Binary division: Guild-Lands (brilliant light, civilized) versus Black Veins (absolute darkness, frontier).

defaultCategoryValues.lighting: Brilliant Light-Crystals (Guild-Lands) vs Absolute Darkness (Black Veins)

visual_details: Colossal Light-Crystals suspended in cavern centers, providing perpetual illumination
Cathedral-like caverns with breathtaking precision stonework
Intricate clockwork visible in all infrastructure
Forged deeps: brilliant guildhalls and city-states connected by maintained thoroughfares
Black Veins: absolute, physical darkness that absorbs light
The Clicking Deeps: marked by resonance pillars and whisper-web triplines
ambient_sounds: Rhythmic clang of hammers in Great Forge complexes (Guild-Lands)
Hum of geothermal systems and Light-Crystal arrays
Absolute silence in the Black Veins (oppressive, reality-breaking)
Clicking and psionic pulses from Silent Folk territories
Mechanical precision: gears, pulleys, pumps functioning perfectly
ambient_smells: Hot metal and forge-fire (Guild-Lands)
Clean, recycled air maintained by atmospheric regulators
Absence of smell in absolute darkness (nothing to carry scent)
Faint methane and sulfur in hazardous Black Vein zones

accessRequirements: Pure Principles philosophy acceptance (ideology, not combat)
biomes.notes: Dvergr bio-engineered with noise-filtering neural architecture (Blight-immune). Pure Steel monopoly: 16-fold lamination, no alternatives. Deep Gates: only functional rapid-transit infrastructure (90% non-functional elsewhere). Nidavellir capital: lit by single massive Anvil-Star. Silent Folk: post-human civilization in Clicking Deeps (hive-cities, echo-cant, psionic acoustic). Three-Covenant: No Light, No Voice, No Metal Tools beyond waystation thresholds. Vein-Runners: frontier prospectors in Black Veins (several hundred operating). Glimmering Grottos: Blight-crystal zones with Wild Magic Surge risk.
